content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 8, 1990

Ardith Davis
City Clerk
City of Morro Bay
595 Harbor Street
Morro Bay, CA 93442

Re: Conflict of Interest Code for
The City of Morro Bay
Our File No. I-90-585

Dear Ms. Davis:

This is in response to your September 5, 1990 letter
requesting advice concerning the conflict of interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act ("Act").¹ Your request is one for
general assistance. Accordingly, we are treating it as a request
for informal assistance.²

You have asked the Fair Political Practices Commission (the
"Commission") to review the conflict of interest code for the City
of Morro Bay ("City") and provide you with any suggestions for
improvement.

As you are aware, the Commission is not the code reviewing
body for city agencies. Pursuant to Government Code Section
87011, the Commission has authority over the codes of multi-county
jurisdictional agencies and state agencies only. The following
information is offered to assist you with the amendment process of
your code and is based solely on the documentation submitted with
your request. We are unable to provide assistance with the
designation or

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Ardith Davis
Page 2

appropriate assignment of disclosure categories as no job duty
statements or organizational charts were submitted.

In conducting a cursory review of your code, there are some
amendments we suggest be made to update the code to conform to
current requirements.

Your code does not provide for disclosure of financial
interests by consultants for the agency. The definition of
designated employee includes consultants. (Section 82019.)
A consultant is any natural person who provides, under contract,
information, advice, recommendation or counsel to any agency.
(Regulation 18700.)

The Commission realizes that not all consultants participate
in the making of decisions on behalf of public agencies and rather
than amend your code each time you retain a consultant that is in
a decision-making capacity, you may use a specialized disclosure
category that provides that the disclosure required of consultants
shall be determined on a case-by-case basis.

Conflict of interest codes should not include reference to
positions specified by Government Code Section 87200. Appendix A
of your code lists both statutorily designated filers as well as
code filers. As the positions of Mayor, Councilmembers, Planning
Commissioners, City Administrator, City Attorney, and Finance
Director are statutorily required to file,³ it is unnecessary to
list them in your city's code. Only those additional positions
within the city should be listed in the code. If the inclusion of
the above-mentioned filers was intended to be of assistance to the
public, perhaps an easier method would be to include this
information as a footnote for reference. Also, the incorporation
cover page of your code should be amended to reflect this change.

Additionally, the version of Regulation 18730 which you have
attached as the body of your code is outdated. I have enclosed
the current version of Regulation 18730 for your reference.
Regulation 18730 as it now reads requires (1) persons assuming
office to disclose, in their statement of economic interest,
income received during the 12 months prior to the filing of the
statement, and (2) persons filing an initial statement, under a
newly-adopted or amended conflict of interest code, to disclose
income received during the 12 months prior to the effective date
of the code. Prior to this amendment, income was not an item

___________________________
³ Government Code Section 87200 specifies positions which are
statutorily required to file Form 721 Statement of Economic
Interests. Positions specifically designated in a city's conflict
of interest code should file Form 730 Statement of Economic
Interests.

Ardith Davis
Page 3

required to be reported on either the assuming office statement or
the initial statement of economic interest. Regulation 18730 was
amended to conform it to the provisions of Section 87302, as
amended effective January 1, 1990.

In an effort to assist you in making these amendments, I have
provided a sample of how your conflict of interest should appear.
The items discussed above have been incorporated in this sample.

Appendix B of your code includes "definitions of terms." If
a definition should be amended statutorily, the city would be
required to amend its code to bring it current with the new law.
We would suggest deleting the first paragraph of Appendix B as
well as the footnote. You may choose to include this language in
a memoranda to personnel or a personnel handbook.

I am enclosing sample language which is used in creating
disclosure categories for conflict of interest codes for multi-county or state agencies. We would suggest the city use Example 1
for all of its current Category 1 filers. You may also wish to
replace the other categories with the language in Examples 2 and 3
for other designated positions.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Staff Services Analyst
Legal Division

:ld

Enclosure
